conditions: Heart failure with preserved ejection fraction (HFpEF)
medications: Nitroglycerin sublingual spray as needed for angina
Salbutamol (albuterol) inhaler as needed before hard sessions
Omeprazole 20 mg daily (gastroprotection while on anticoagulation)
health.mental_health: Generally resilient and purpose-driven, but experiences periodic anxiety about recurrence of cardiac symptoms and occasional sleep disruption after night shifts; uses structured training plans and brief mindfulness exercises to cope.
recent_visits: Cardiology follow-up at Charité – Universitätsmedizin Berlin (med review, training clearance)
Holter monitor placement and results review for intermittent palpitations
Sports medicine appointment for knee pain and training load adjustment
Physiotherapy sessions for knee strengthening and mobility

finance.debt: Small remaining mortgage/loan on a Berlin apartment; prefers to keep it for stability despite nearing payoff.
investments: Emergency cash buffer in a Tagesgeld account
recent_transactions: €420 payment to a private sports cardiology stress test not fully covered by insurance
€160 monthly physiotherapy co-payments and sports massage
€210 purchase of a chest-strap heart-rate monitor and replacement sensors
€95 entry fee for a masters athletics competition in Brandenburg
€780 annual premium for liability and household insurance renewal

relationships: Partner: Jonas Krüger (lives together in Prenzlauer Berg; supportive but worries about overtraining after the cardiac event)
Training group: masters track club at Sportforum Hohenschönhausen (tight-knit, performance-focused peers)
Close friend: Derya Yılmaz (colleague in the ED; occasional training buddy for easy runs)
Sibling: Tunde Adeyemi (lives in the UK; regular calls, sometimes tense around health decisions and retirement plans)
social.social_media_activity: Uses Strava and a private Instagram account to share race results and training milestones; avoids posting hospital-related content and keeps most posts limited to friends due to workplace privacy concerns.
private_conversations: Discussions with partner about end-of-life preferences and advance directives after the prior cardiac event
Conversations with cardiologist about anticoagulation risks and whether to reduce competition intensity
Workplace-related debriefs about traumatic ED cases and how they affect sleep and training
Gender identity boundaries at work and in the athletics community (pronouns, changing-room comfort)

schedule.work_hours: Full-time rotating shifts in an emergency department (approx. 36–40 hours/week): mix of early (07:00–15:30), late (14:30–23:00), and occasional nights (22:30–07:30), plus 1–2 weekends/month.
regular_appointments: Cardiology check-in every 3–6 months (Charité outpatient clinic)
Masters track club sessions Tue/Thu evenings
Monthly anticoagulation/medication review with Hausarzt (GP) when needed
upcoming_events: Masters 5,000 m race at Mommsenstadion (Berlin) next month
Follow-up echocardiogram and stress test scheduled in 6 weeks
ED mandatory training day on triage updates and resuscitation refreshers
schedule.daily_routine: Plans training around shift pattern: easy aerobic work after early shifts, quality sessions on off-days, and strength/mobility most mornings; tracks sleep and HRV closely and prioritizes recovery meals before commuting to the hospital.

professional.company: Large public university hospital emergency department in Berlin (Charité-affiliated setting)
projects: Leading a small quality-improvement effort on reducing door-to-ECG time for chest pain patients
Mentoring two newly hired nurses on trauma bay workflow and documentation standards
Updating unit training materials on anticoagulant bleeding protocols (informed by own medication knowledge)
professional.performance_issues: Occasional fatigue after night shifts can reduce tolerance for back-to-back high-acuity cases; has requested more predictable scheduling around major competitions to avoid overexertion, which sometimes creates tension with staffing needs.
workplace_relationships: Strong rapport with ED charge nurse (Anke Hoffmann) who supports flexible scheduling when possible
Collaborative relationship with cardiology liaison team for rapid consults
Keeps professional distance from a coworker who makes dismissive comments about masters athletics and age

hobbies: Cycling along Tempelhofer Feld on recovery days
Sauna visits (carefully timed around hydration and anticoagulation)
Cooking West African and Mediterranean-inspired heart-healthy meals
Listening to medical podcasts and athletics coaching analyses
habits: Uses a wearable and chest-strap HR monitor; logs training, sleep, and symptoms daily
Limits alcohol to rare occasions due to atrial fibrillation triggers
Caffeine strategically timed for shifts; avoids it after 16:00 when not on nights
Carries nitroglycerin and a medical ID card in training bag
Occasionally uses over-the-counter topical NSAID gel for knee pain (avoids oral NSAIDs due to anticoagulation)
lifestyle.living_situation: Lives with partner in a quiet two-bedroom apartment in Prenzlauer Berg; keeps a dedicated corner for a stationary bike, foam roller, and resistance bands to train when shifts make outdoor sessions impractical.
lifestyle.dietary_preferences: Heart-healthy, high-protein approach: lots of vegetables, legumes, fish, and whole grains; moderate sodium; times carbs around harder sessions; careful hydration and electrolyte intake to reduce cramping and arrhythmia risk.
